synthetic_answers.answer: Yes. She is scheduled for thyroidectomy for Graves disease, has biochemically confirmed hyperthyroidism, and is receiving antithyroid medication.
She can provide signed informed consent, is not pregnant, has no planned concurrent parathyroidectomy, lymph node clearance, or redo surgery, and the surgical indication is Graves disease.
No. The clinician confirms she has never received platinum chemotherapy and has had no prior systemic cancer therapy.

No active interstitial lung disease, uncontrolled cardiac disease, active uncontrolled infection, or organ transplant is present. Her Graves disease is active and should be treated as active autoimmune disease for exclusion screening in non-Graves trials.
No. There is no history of cancer diagnosis or cancer survivorship at age 39 or younger.

synthetic_answers.answer: Yes. He has moderate to severe active thyroid eye disease, Clinical Activity Score 4 in the study eye, and ocular symptoms that began 8 months before screening.

No relevant exclusion is present. He has not received anti-IGF-1R therapy, recent corticosteroids, selenium, immunosuppression, investigational therapy, or radioactive iodine; he has had no orbital irradiation or decompression, no abnormal hearing issue, no inflammatory bowel disease, and no interfering ophthalmic condition.

For the IBI3031 criteria, weight is 82 kg, Clinical Activity Score is 4, symptom duration is within 12 months, ophthalmic findings are qualifying, thyroid function is acceptable, and TRAb is positive. There is no dysthyroid optic neuropathy, corneal ulcer, non-TED ophthalmic disease affecting interpretation, severe thyroid dysfunction, poorly controlled diabetes, or poorly controlled hypertension.
No. He has not received platinum chemotherapy.

No active interstitial lung disease, uncontrolled cardiac disease, active uncontrolled infection, or organ transplant is present. His thyroid eye disease is active and should be treated as active autoimmune disease for off-target immunotherapy exclusion screening.
No. There is no cancer diagnosis or cancer survivorship history.

synthetic_answers.answer: Yes. He has Graves disease with progressive moderate to severe thyroid eye disease, BMI 25 kg/m2, acceptable thyroid function, best corrected visual acuity 20/25, and qualifying proptosis and Clinical Activity Score. He has no diabetes, serious non-TED ocular disease, unsafe medical or psychiatric condition, recent malignancy therapy, relevant allergy, vaccination timing conflict, immediate orbital intervention need, prior TED surgery, or recent investigational trial participation.

Yes. He has active TED with Clinical Activity Score 4, symptom duration within 12 months, qualifying ophthalmic findings, weight 78 kg, acceptable thyroid function, and positive TRAb. He has no dysthyroid optic neuropathy, corneal ulcer, non-TED ophthalmic disease affecting interpretation, severe thyroid dysfunction, poorly controlled diabetes, or poorly controlled hypertension.

Yes. He meets active TED severity, Clinical Activity Score, and onset timing for the VRDN-003 trial and has no prior anti-IGF-1R therapy, recent steroids, selenium, immunosuppressive treatment, investigational therapy, radioactive iodine, orbital irradiation or decompression, hearing issue, inflammatory bowel disease, or interfering ophthalmic condition.

He has not received platinum chemotherapy. He has no active interstitial lung disease, uncontrolled cardiac disease, active uncontrolled infection, or organ transplant. His thyroid eye disease is active and should be treated as active autoimmune disease for off-target immunotherapy exclusion screening.

synthetic_answers.answer: She has active thyroid-associated ophthalmopathy with Clinical Activity Score 5 and maintained euthyroidism, and she is not pregnant. She has not received three doses of intravenous methylprednisolone, so poor response after that treatment cannot be met. She also has severe hypertension and no diabetic retinopathy.

For the IBI3031 criteria, she has active TED-equivalent disease with Clinical Activity Score 5, qualifying symptom duration and ophthalmic findings, weight 61 kg, acceptable thyroid function, and positive TRAb. However, severe hypertension is present.

She has not received platinum chemotherapy. She has no active interstitial lung disease, uncontrolled cardiac disease, active uncontrolled infection, or organ transplant. Her thyroid-associated ophthalmopathy is active and should be treated as active autoimmune disease for off-target immunotherapy exclusion screening.

synthetic_answers.answer: Yes. She has refractory Graves disease after more than 3 years of antithyroid drug therapy with repeated relapse after withdrawal, and serum TRAb is positive.

She can consent and comply, agrees to contraception if required, and has no severe drug allergy, recent lipid nanoparticle therapy, recent live vaccine, malignancy history, positive exclusionary infectious serologies, severe psychiatric or cognitive impairment, or hematologic dysfunction.
No. She has never received platinum chemotherapy.

She has no active interstitial lung disease. Her refractory Graves disease is active and should be treated as active autoimmune disease for off-target trial exclusion screening. The initial note already reports no uncontrolled cardiac disease, no active uncontrolled infection, and no organ transplant.
